question: What is the primary purpose of an index?
options: To store the table's data permanently
To speed up row lookups by avoiding full table scans (like a book's index)
To force a COMMIT
To compress the table

explanation.uz: Indeks — kitob indeksi kabi: kerakli qatorni tez topish uchun. U to'liq jadval skanini (full table scan) o'rniga aniq bloklarga yo'naltiradi. Lekin DML'ni sekinlashtiradi (indeks ham yangilanadi).
explanation.en: An index, like a book's index, speeds up finding rows — it avoids a full table scan by pointing to the right blocks. It does slow down DML, since the index must be maintained too.

question: What is the DEFAULT index type created by CREATE INDEX in Oracle?
options: Bitmap
B-tree
Hash
Reverse key

explanation.uz: Standart indeks — B-tree (balanced tree). U keng ko'lamli, ayniqsa yuqori kardinallikdagi (ko'p turli qiymatli) ustunlar va OLTP uchun mos.
explanation.en: The default index is a B-tree (balanced tree). It is general-purpose and works well for high-cardinality columns (many distinct values) and OLTP.

question: Why are BITMAP indexes usually a poor choice on tables with heavy concurrent DML (OLTP)?
options: They cannot be created on such tables
A single row change can lock a whole bitmap segment (covering many rows), causing serious concurrency problems
They are always larger than B-tree indexes
They cannot index NULLs

explanation.uz: Bitmap indeksda bitta qatorni o'zgartirish ko'p qatorni qamrab oladigan butun bitmap bo'lagini bloklaydi — bu OLTP'da (ko'p parallel DML) jiddiy konkurentlik muammosi. Shuning uchun bitmap asosan DWH / o'qishga mo'ljallangan tizimlarda ishlatiladi.
explanation.en: In a bitmap index, changing one row can lock an entire bitmap segment that covers many rows — a serious concurrency problem under OLTP (lots of concurrent DML). Hence bitmaps are used mainly in DWH / read-mostly systems.

question: What does CREATE UNIQUE INDEX enforce?
options: Faster inserts
That the indexed column(s) contain no duplicate values
Automatic compression
That the column is NOT NULL

explanation.uz: Unique indeks indekslangan ustun(lar)da takror qiymatlarga yo'l qo'ymaydi. PRIMARY KEY va UNIQUE cheklovlari aynan shunday indeks orqali ta'minlanadi. (NULL'ga esa ruxsat beriladi.)
explanation.en: A unique index disallows duplicate values in the indexed column(s). PRIMARY KEY and UNIQUE constraints are enforced by exactly such an index. (NULLs are still allowed.)

question: What is a REVERSE KEY index and why use one?
options: It stores the table rows in reverse order
It reverses the bytes of the key so sequential values scatter across leaf blocks — reducing 'hot block' contention on monotonically increasing keys (e.g. a sequence)
It reverses the query result
It removes duplicate keys

explanation.uz: Reverse key indeks kalit baytlarini teskari qiladi — shuning uchun ketma-ket o'sadigan qiymatlar (masalan sequence'dan) turli leaf bloklarga tarqaladi. Bu 'issiq blok' (hot block) uchun konkurentlikni kamaytiradi (RAC'da ayniqsa foydali).
explanation.en: A reverse key index reverses the bytes of the key, so monotonically increasing values (e.g. from a sequence) scatter across leaf blocks. This reduces 'hot block' contention (especially useful in RAC).

question: On a partitioned table, what is the difference between a LOCAL and a GLOBAL index?
options: There is no difference
A LOCAL index is partitioned to match the table (one index partition per table partition); a GLOBAL index is a single structure spanning all partitions
A local index is always faster
A global index cannot be unique

explanation.uz: LOCAL indeks jadval bilan bir xil bo'linadi — har jadval bo'lagiga bitta indeks bo'lagi. Shuning uchun bo'lakni tashlash/qo'shish oson. GLOBAL indeks esa barcha bo'laklarni qamrab oluvchi yagona struktura — bo'lak o'zgarsa qayta qurish kerak bo'lishi mumkin.
explanation.en: A LOCAL index is partitioned the same way as the table — one index partition per table partition, so dropping/adding a partition is easy. A GLOBAL index is one structure spanning all partitions and may need rebuilding when partitions change.

question: You add a PRIMARY KEY. Can Oracle reuse an already-existing index on the same columns instead of creating a new one?
options: No — a PK always creates its own new index
Yes — if a suitable index (unique or non-unique) on those columns already exists, the PK can use it
Only if that index is a bitmap
Only in a data warehouse

explanation.uz: Ha. Agar o'sha ustun(lar) bo'yicha mos indeks (unique yoki hatto non-unique) allaqachon mavjud bo'lsa, PRIMARY KEY yangi indeks yaratmasdan o'shani ishlatadi. Bu ortiqcha indeksni oldini oladi.
explanation.en: Yes. If a suitable index (unique, or even non-unique) on those columns already exists, the PRIMARY KEY uses it instead of creating a new one — avoiding a redundant index.

question: After ALTER TABLE ... MOVE (relocating a table's rows), what happens to that table's indexes?
options: Nothing — they keep working
They become UNUSABLE and must be rebuilt, because the rows' physical rowids changed
They are automatically dropped
They shrink automatically

explanation.uz: MOVE qatorlarni jismonan ko'chiradi — rowid'lar o'zgaradi. Indekslar rowid'ga tayangani uchun ular UNUSABLE holatga o'tadi va ALTER INDEX ... REBUILD bilan qayta qurilishi shart.
explanation.en: MOVE physically relocates rows, so their rowids change. Because indexes point by rowid, they become UNUSABLE and must be rebuilt with ALTER INDEX ... REBUILD.

question: You RENAME a table. What happens to its indexes and constraints?
options: They become unusable
They remain valid and continue to work (rename doesn't move data or change rowids)
They are dropped
They must be recreated

explanation.uz: RENAME faqat nomni o'zgartiradi — ma'lumot ko'chmaydi, rowid'lar o'zgarmaydi. Shuning uchun indekslar va cheklovlar amal qilaveradi (MOVE'dan farqli o'laroq).
explanation.en: RENAME only changes the name — data isn't moved and rowids don't change. So indexes and constraints stay valid and keep working (unlike MOVE).

question: On a busy PRODUCTION system, how do you create or rebuild an index with minimal blocking of DML?
options: Plain CREATE INDEX / ALTER INDEX REBUILD (these lock the table)
Use the ONLINE clause: CREATE INDEX ... ONLINE or ALTER INDEX ... REBUILD ONLINE, which allows concurrent DML
Drop the table first, then recreate it
It is impossible on a live system

explanation.uz: Oddiy CREATE INDEX / REBUILD jadvalni bloklaydi (DML to'xtaydi). Prod'da ONLINE bandidan foydalaniladi — CREATE INDEX ... ONLINE yoki ALTER INDEX ... REBUILD ONLINE — bu parallel DML'ga ruxsat beradi.
explanation.en: A plain CREATE INDEX / REBUILD locks the table (blocking DML). On production use the ONLINE clause — CREATE INDEX ... ONLINE or ALTER INDEX ... REBUILD ONLINE — which allows concurrent DML.
